239511.explanation: However, the system must be revised to prevent political parties from using it as a tool in the future.

239512.explanation: The best maintenance of our municipal greenery is done best together. After all, residents see it daily and know what is going on (literally).

239513.explanation: The municipality has an executive role here, but this happens with support and input from the neighborhood. And yes, that must be improved!

239514.explanation: As soon as this can be done by working smartly as a municipality and thinking outside the box, certainly do it.

239515.explanation: The tourist tax is high enough; it just needs to be collected properly.

239516.explanation: Our municipality must be an entrepreneurial municipality, working together with entrepreneurs.

239517.explanation: The existing site can be expanded. There is no room for more.

239518.explanation: It is already almost impossible for farmers to continue farming. We have farmers who are also nature managers, and that has worked well for centuries.

239519.explanation: This makes farming sustainable for the future.

239522.explanation: Landsmeer has the longest waiting list in the Netherlands. It's high time for our own starters, families, and seniors.

239523.explanation: This can be done perfectly through our excellent network in the municipality.

239524.explanation: Possibly with an active burning advisory.

239526.explanation: We stand for all Landsmeer businesses; sustainability is the future for every company. Local Landsmeer wants the municipality to provide practical and financial support in this.

239527.explanation: We want to help individuals preserve the tradition as much as possible by continuing to facilitate an accessible option for firework lighting.

239528.explanation: Our beautiful green environment must not be polluted by this.

239529.explanation: The Twiske is a recreational area. With the permits currently granted, the limit has been reached.

239533.explanation: This is becoming more urgently needed.

239534.explanation: Local Landsmeer stands for a municipality where we are safe and feel safe.

239535.explanation: Local Landsmeer has already achieved a solid and fair distribution between social classes and others during the past term, and we see sufficient balance there.

239536.explanation: As long as it remains affordable, with priority for Landsmeer, Ilpen and Purmerland residents, and also suitable Waterland-type housing.

239537.explanation: The housing shortage is too great to deviate from this any longer.

239539.explanation: Transformation can also help provide more homes for Ilpen, Landsmeer and Purmerland residents.

239540.explanation: This is the only way to add more homes for our residents. Hands off the Landsmeerderveld, Ilperveld and 't Twiske.

239541.explanation: This also helps starters counter the housing shortage, but it should remain an exception.

239545.explanation: The retail sector in Landsmeer is under pressure; the municipality must always look for ways to optimally maintain the business climate in our municipality, including sufficient (free) parking.

239547.explanation: After the neighborhood inspection, Local Landsmeer became clear: it has become a mess now that Amsterdam has once again shifted the problems over the fence. Local Landsmeer wants a permit for Ilpen, Landsmeer and Purmerland residents for the entire municipality, and blue zones for “guests”.

239549.explanation: The youth of Landsmeer are the future.

239550.explanation: There should be broader communication about everything available within our municipality for those aged 67 and over.

239556.explanation: These are meaningful sustainable investments that increase our biodiversity.

239557.explanation: This also helps counter the housing shortage.

239558.explanation: Residents who need help must be able to get it easily.

239559.explanation: The gap must be closed and noise-reducing asphalt applied; this combination will solve the problems.

239560.explanation: At this moment there is little nuisance; Schiphol is important for our economy and is rapidly becoming more sustainable.

239564.explanation: Local Landsmeer wants to use the app more broadly so residents can access more services there.

239565.explanation: A residential area is planned in a released part of the field. If it concerns the road that must run through there, then “agreed.” If it concerns another part of the field, Local Landsmeer says “hands off our green environment.”

239511.explanation: With a referendum, we involve our residents in local democracy. The municipality of Landsmeer already has a referendum ordinance.

239512.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA wants to commit to green and healthy neighborhoods. Taking direct control of the local environment promotes residents’ involvement in the neighborhood.

239513.explanation: We want the municipality to facilitate initiatives from the community that lead to neighborhood improvement.

239514.explanation: Besides a contribution from the national municipal fund, the municipality also receives income from property tax. The level of this tax is comparable to other municipalities in the region. We see no reason to lower it now.

239515.explanation: Tourists staying in Landsmeer contribute through the tourist tax to maintaining the quality of Landsmeer. With this income, we make Landsmeer worth visiting.

239516.explanation: Landsmeer is already an attractive location for businesses. A promotion campaign would create an undesirable shortage.

239517.explanation: The territory of Landsmeer is zoned for housing, green areas, sports, nature and business parks. More business parks would limit the more preferred uses for us.

239518.explanation: Vacated farmland retains its current function as cultural-historical heritage and habitat for flora and fauna. Converting it into a nature reserve fits perfectly with the current situation.

239519.explanation: Farmers should be able to develop small-scale activities alongside their farming business.

239522.explanation: The housing market in Landsmeer is tight. We would like half of the available social rental properties to be allocated first to local housing seekers, and the rest afterwards based on urgency.

239523.explanation: Newcomers bring added value to society after integration. The municipality must facilitate this as well as possible.

239524.explanation: GreenLeft-PvdA wants to allow the use of fireplaces and wood stoves in Landsmeer for part of the year. Because we are concerned about public health and the environment, we want stricter monitoring of compliance with the RIVM’s burning guidelines. At certain times of the year, it will not be allowed to use fireplaces and wood stoves.

239525.explanation: The municipality should provide a volunteer allowance to persons who want to care for part of the neighborhood’s green maintenance.

239526.explanation: The municipality of Landsmeer must assess sustainability when a company requests to establish itself.

239527.explanation: The municipality is responsible for granting permits and/or organizing the celebration of the start of the new year. On New Year's Eve from midnight, there must be space in the centers of Purmerland, Den Ilp, and Landsmeer for light shows with adjusted sound effects.

239528.explanation: The meadows in the municipality have a protected status as breeding grounds for meadow birds (Natura 2000) or as cultural-historical landscapes. Because of this protected status, no solar panels may be installed.

239529.explanation: In the coming administrative period, the event policy of the Twiske-Waterland recreation authority will be evaluated. If it appears that the Natura 2000 nature objectives are not being met, GroenLinks-PvdA advocates reducing the number of festival days.

239531.explanation: Landsmeer is a water-rich municipality. Learning to swim should be mandatory and is best organized through primary education. The municipality should provide financial support if desired by the school boards.

239533.explanation: The municipality of Landsmeer has many rules. Enforcement must continue, even outside municipal office hours.

239534.explanation: The feeling of safety in public spaces is enhanced by installing cameras. The currently limited presence may, according to GroenLinks-PvdA, be significantly expanded.

239535.explanation: The need for social rental homes in the municipality of Landsmeer is enormous. A 40% proportion in a mix with private and authorized institutions is acceptable. In projects by housing corporations and the municipality, this percentage should be doubled. GroenLinks-PvdA will strictly monitor this.

239536.explanation: In Landsmeer, within the central areas, the maximum number of building floors is currently four. To preserve the character of the village, this is the maximum. Outside the center, the maximum is three floors.

239537.explanation: Houses are meant to be lived in. Recreational rental and temporary subletting to company employees are undesirable in our view.

239539.explanation: Vacant commercial buildings (farms) may be converted with subsidies from the municipality if, after completion, they contribute to the visual quality of the environment or the preservation of historical heritage.

239540.explanation: Within the built-up area, there is sufficient space to realize the upcoming housing task. We also count the strips directly on both sides of the ribbon developments as part of the built-up area.

239541.explanation: We welcome experimentation with housing forms by housing corporations. Private landlords are not authorized institutions and are not supervised; therefore, we only want to give room to the corporations.

239542.explanation: Not everyone can participate fully in society. GroenLinks-PvdA calls for extra attention to this group of residents and wants nobody to live in poverty. If raising the income limit helps reduce this group, then it should be done. We stand for solidarity.

239543.explanation: It is important that people receiving social assistance have a meaningful role in society. Linking this role to their benefits is, in our view, highly undesirable.

239545.explanation: With the creation of blue zones in the center, there is sufficient parking space for the shops in the area.

239547.explanation: There is no paid parking in Landsmeer. Parking permits cannot be introduced here.

239549.explanation: To reach the large group of young people in Landsmeer, the number of hours must be increased.

239550.explanation: For pensioners with a low income and little wealth, it can be helpful if the municipality provides financial support when there is a need to attend cultural activities.

239556.explanation: Initiatives from residents must be honored. GroenLinks-PvdA stands for green and healthy neighborhoods.

239557.explanation: In Landsmeer, different forms of housing can be experimented with. The need for small homes is great, so sharing or splitting larger single-family homes is a good thing.

239558.explanation: Applying for allowances and benefits is quite complicated for many residents. The help of a social counselor can then be wise.

239559.explanation: A part is missing in the noise barrier along the A10. We support (including financially) the construction of this missing section and expect that noise nuisance from road traffic in the adjacent part of Luijedijk-Zuid will then be reduced to an acceptable level.

239560.explanation: Reducing the number of flight movements to and from Schiphol is of great importance for the region.

239561.explanation: Fast food is unhealthy. In locations regularly frequented by youth, we believe advertising for fast food should not be allowed.

239564.explanation: The quality of the immediate living environment benefits from controlling the level of pollution. Taking quicker action on reports improves quality.

239565.explanation: Any disturbance of the Landsmeerderveld, especially the construction of an access road, is a violation of the centuries-old (700 years) cultural-historical landscape and the area's habitat for flora and fauna.

239511.explanation: D66 is the party of democratic renewal. We like to give residents a voice on major decisions through, for example, referenda, but we prefer to combine this with more creative forms of participation and innovative citizen councils for real dialogue.

239512.explanation: D66 Landsmeer is pleased with involved residents with green thumbs. When neighbors jointly maintain a public garden, it not only improves the living environment but also strengthens social cohesion in the neighborhood.

239513.explanation: Small ideas often have a big impact. D66 Landsmeer wants residents with good plans for their street or neighborhood to be supported by the municipality. Together we make Landsmeer more beautiful.

239514.explanation: D66 chooses fair choices. If financially possible, D66 Landsmeer would like to reduce the financial burden for residents. But this may not lead to cuts or reversal of investments in, for example, education or greenery.

239515.explanation: Visitors enjoy our beautiful nature and facilities. D66 finds it fair that they contribute via tourist tax to the maintenance of our municipality. This tax is currently in line with surrounding municipalities.

239516.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes that limited financial resources should be spent carefully. Being an attractive location for businesses is not our priority.

239517.explanation: Space in Landsmeer is scarce. D66 Landsmeer prioritizes housing construction and nature. We prefer to focus on making current industrial areas more sustainable and smarter used.

239518.explanation: D66 Landsmeer sees our farmers as indispensable managers of our landscape. We encourage the transition to nature-inclusive agriculture, where food production and nature go hand in hand.

239519.explanation: To remain future-proof, farmers must be able to operate. D66 Landsmeer supports farm shops or small-scale camping, provided the village and rural character is preserved.

239522.explanation: Everyone deserves a fair chance. D66 Landsmeer chooses more social housing for all target groups, so that we do not have to pit housing seekers against each other.

239523.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes investment must be made in language and work for newcomers. Rapid integration prevents isolation and is the best path to a strong, connected society. The municipality of Landsmeer currently offers various programs to integrate newcomers.

239524.explanation: Wood burning contributes to poor air quality, but D66 Landsmeer is not in favor of a total ban. However, under certain weather conditions, the smoke lingers more and affects others more. We would like to see this taken into account.

239525.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes that resident involvement should be encouraged and, where possible, facilitated. This can also be done by making a (small) budget available.

239526.explanation: The future is green. D66 Landsmeer assists entrepreneurs with the transition, but a requirement that only companies operating sustainably may establish themselves in Landsmeer seems unrealistic.

239527.explanation: Festive and safe. D66 Landsmeer gladly trades the damage and nuisance caused by fireworks for a professional, centralized show where we as a municipality celebrate the New Year together.

239528.explanation: D66 Landsmeer supports clean energy generation but believes that meadows should no longer be freely covered with solar panels. The preference is to cover roofs as much as possible and only use agricultural and natural land if no other option is available.

239529.explanation: Although Het Twiske originally is a recreational area with natural value-an environment with more possibilities than a protected nature reserve-D66 Landsmeer believes the balance between festivals and nature must be well maintained. That balance exists with the current number of festivals.

239531.explanation: Landsmeer is a water-rich municipality and therefore D66 Landsmeer strongly supports school swimming, provided it is practically feasible for schools to organize it.

239533.explanation: If there is a reason, D66 Landsmeer believes that supervision officers (boa's) should also be deployable outside office hours. However, we do not see a need for continuous, 24-hour deployment.

239534.explanation: An important part of safety is feeling free and protecting privacy. D66 is therefore cautious about deploying camera surveillance and preventive searches. Such (temporary) measures should only be approved if there is a clear necessity.

239535.explanation: The waiting list for home seekers is long, so it is good that there is a balance between social rental homes, mid-range rent, and private sector homes in new construction. In our view, this can be looked at in totality, not necessarily per project.

239536.explanation: More than four floors is considered by D66 Landsmeer not to fit the village and rural character of the municipality.

239537.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes it should be prevented that investors buy existing owner-occupied homes and then split and rent them out at high prices. To give home seekers more chances for an affordable home to buy, we support implementing a buy-back protection (owner-occupancy obligation).

239539.explanation: Converting properties into homes is a good idea according to D66 Landsmeer, as it increases housing availability. But a subsidy from the municipality is not necessary for this.

239540.explanation: It has become apparent that there is high demand for homes at the entrance to Landsmeer. For D66 Landsmeer, new construction outside the built-up area must compensate for any loss of green space. We believe Landsmeer should remain a green and rural municipality.

239541.explanation: D66 Landsmeer supports converting larger homes into smaller one-person residences to meet demand. However, it must fit into the neighborhood and not cause nuisance to residents.

239542.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes the municipality should provide tailored solutions regardless of income limits. The national government should ensure an adequate social minimum, for example by raising the minimum wage and social assistance. This prevents large differences between municipalities.

239543.explanation: D66 Landsmeer thinks each case should be reviewed individually to determine the possibilities for a social assistance recipient to perform paid work or volunteer work.

239545.explanation: D66 Landsmeer sees no room to create more parking spaces here. With the introduction of the blue zone at the Market Square, there are sufficient free parking spaces for shoppers. Our priority is safe cycling, walking traffic, and pedestrians.

239547.explanation: We observe that with the introduction of paid parking in Amsterdam North, parking pressure in Landsmeer has increased. D66 Landsmeer wants an investigation into the size of this problem and the necessary solutions.

239549.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes the current youth work is effective. This is important for contact with young people to prevent neighborhood nuisance and loneliness among youth. If more than 20 hours is needed for this, we are open to it. Preferably, we would see a youth meeting place established.

239550.explanation: D66 Landsmeer wants everyone to have the chance to participate in cultural activities and supports a city pass for those who cannot afford it. A city pass can also otherwise lower barriers to participation in cultural activities.

239556.explanation: D66 Landsmeer would like the municipality to support such initiatives and facilitate them where possible. This does not necessarily have to be financial support but can also take the form of tools or plants.

239557.explanation: D66 Landsmeer supports ideas and initiatives in housing to respond to different needs. For example, larger homes can be replaced by or converted into smaller one-person homes. This must fit within the neighborhood and not cause nuisance.

239558.explanation: The social counselor should be easily accessible. This can be through extra walk-in hours but could also be by phone or appointment.

239559.explanation: Residents of Luijendijk-Zuid have been requesting for years to close the 'gap' in the noise barriers along the A10. Plans have been made to place a so-called Whiswall, which deflects sound. We hope this provides a good solution.

239560.explanation: Quality of the living environment is more important than Schiphol's growth. Especially in summer, air traffic causes nuisance in the municipality. Considering the climate and nitrogen crises and requests for farmers to reduce operations, D66 Landsmeer supports revising the airspace.

239561.explanation: We oppose nannying, but a healthy environment aids healthy choices. D66 Landsmeer does not want temptations for unhealthy food in places where many children come, such as sports parks and bus shelters.

239564.explanation: Reports must indeed be addressed quickly if they pose a danger. We must manage limited capacity and financial resources carefully, and it is questionable whether reports need to be handled even faster.

239565.explanation: D66 Landsmeer believes a solution is needed for the traffic congestion on the Lint but does not support a road through a nature reserve.

239511.explanation: The Landsmeer VVD opposes referendums and believes more in our representative democracy. Many issues cannot be reduced to yes/no questions. Important decisions also require thorough and well-founded information and consideration.

239512.explanation: Self-management is a good way to increase residents' involvement in their own living environment.

239513.explanation: They already can do that, and therefore they can continue to receive funding for it.

239514.explanation: If there is room for it, it should certainly be considered. The property tax should not be a default lever that is turned locally to cover expenditures.

239515.explanation: The tourist tax is high enough and, due to its relative nature (7%), automatically moves with the economic cycle. Tourism entrepreneurs already face difficulties due to the recent VAT increase on overnight stays.

239516.explanation: In an appropriate manner and by smart regional cooperation and taking the (administrative) role within the Amsterdam Metropolitan Area, as is currently done. For example, within the regional collaboration with Bureau Tourism Low Holland.

239517.explanation: There is still space for companies and other facilities in the western entrance area on Scheepsbouwersweg. Without companies, there is no thriving local economy. Companies are important for our amenities.

239518.explanation: It is inevitable that in certain places close to nature reserves, agricultural land will be converted into nature, and farmers will become nature managers. However, agricultural land remains important for maintaining that same nature and for the survival of the agricultural entrepreneur.

239519.explanation: Especially in case of downsizing the business operations.

239522.explanation: Status holders are one of the focus groups within housing. The municipality has a legal annual assignment to house a certain number of status holders; no prioritization is needed for this.

239523.explanation: The municipality spends sufficiently on the integration of newcomers through the local implementation of the Participation Act. Involvement/support of organizations from civil society (church, welfare, community centers) can contribute to this.

239524.explanation: Within the scope allowed by European regulations.

239525.explanation: The municipality already provides funds for citizens who want to maintain greenery in their neighborhood themselves. Rules have been established for this. It is good if this possibility continues to exist.

239526.explanation: What does "sustainable" mean in this context? What does this mean for companies already established in the municipality? How will we measure this? It should be clear that we do not want to attract polluting companies to our municipality.

239527.explanation: In our view, this is not a municipal task.

239528.explanation: The municipality should not actively look for space for solar panels in the rural area. However, (small-scale) situations may arise where solar panels are an interesting addition to the energy mix. Think of joint generation in the sports park.

239529.explanation: The number of permitted noise days in Het Twiske has already been capped at five per year. That is sufficient. Moreover, the municipality should not decide this unilaterally, as it shares the management costs of Het Twiske with other municipalities.

239531.explanation: We wonder if there is demand for this and additionally find this primarily the responsibility of schools. There are sufficient opportunities in Landsmeer to learn to swim. In cases of insurmountable financial barriers, recourse can be made to the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture.

239533.explanation: That is the ideal situation, but is it financially realistic?

239534.explanation: There are sufficient possibilities for camera surveillance in public spaces if the situation calls for it.

239535.explanation: In the Housing Care Vision, Landsmeer follows the affordability requirements for housing construction imposed by the national government (e.g., through the Housing Act). We aim for 2/3 affordable (67%) and 30% social rental housing. This fits the population composition of Landsmeer.

239536.explanation: This is conceivable on the edges of (future) expansion locations on the southern/Amsterdam side of the municipality, such as Entree-East. Comparable to, for example, the Poortgebouw in Luijendijk-South. Preferably not in the village center.

239537.explanation: Owner-occupied homes in any case should not be removed from the housing stock. Owner-occupation in the case of new-build homes could help prevent speculation, but buying and renting out homes-for example, to one's own children-should not be prohibited. There is actually a great need for rental homes in the middle segment and the free sector.

239539.explanation: Such transformation does not require subsidies from the municipality. This can be done well by the market or housing corporations.

239540.explanation: New construction outside the built-up area is necessary to meet future housing construction needs. With respect for landscape and cultural history, further expansion at Entree-East is possible, as well as on transformation locations along the village ribbon.

239541.explanation: House splitting or sharing contributes to reducing the housing shortage. Room-by-room rental can-if properly regulated-help increase housing supply; especially in urgent and temporary cases.

239542.explanation: The current limit is low enough.

239543.explanation: Reciprocity is an important cornerstone on which support- in any form- rests.

239545.explanation: There are more than enough parking spaces at the shopping center, especially after the introduction of the blue zone in the Market Square.

239547.explanation: Paid parking should be postponed as long as possible. Parking permits are not necessarily a solution to the (experienced) parking pressure.

239549.explanation: Adequate youth work is more than just clocking hours. With the current 20 hours, a lot is already getting started.

239550.explanation: We do not get the impression that there are financial barriers to participating in cultural activities in the municipality of Landsmeer. The offerings of, for example, SamenMeer and music and cultural associations are (financially) accessible.

239556.explanation: Rules have been established for how residents can maintain greenery in their own neighborhood. Support through providing materials or hardware already takes place. Also, in consultation with housing corporations, contributions are already made to greening neighborhoods.

239557.explanation: House splitting or sharing contributes to reducing the housing shortage. It offers a chance to better match the supply of (single-person) homes to the demand.

239558.explanation: Sufficient opportunities exist within the existing social and community networks.

239559.explanation: The existing gap in the sound barrier near Luijendijk-South must be (auditorily) closed. The municipality is obligated to the residents to do this.

239560.explanation: The municipality must keep a close watch regarding landing and takeoff routes over the municipality.

239561.explanation: This is absolutely not a municipal task!

239564.explanation: The Fixi app is one of the municipal instruments that functions very well and about which residents are very satisfied. The municipality must ensure that this system continues to function well.

239565.explanation: A road through the Landsmeerderveld nature reserve is undesirable. For the purpose of access to the Entree Landsmeer-East development area, a road is inevitable and also offers opportunities to improve the village's accessibility.

239511.explanation: ABSOLUTELY. For example, administrative merger: the Executive Board and majority say: "We are going to Purmerend!" But what do you think? REFERENDUM BEFORE THE ELECTIONS IS SKIPPED!! This choice can cost you a lot of money. Near Amsterdam, your house is worth much more. On average €100,000 (Positieflandsmeer.nl)

239512.explanation: Involvement in the immediate living environment and caring for it partly determines overall well-being. In this way, a public garden truly becomes a part of the neighborhood. Good agreements must be made and a spokesperson appointed. A model agreement could possibly be signed.

239513.explanation: The immediate living environment partly determines whether you live safely and pleasantly. Residents should certainly be given the possibilities and be able to submit a plan. If approved, an amount should also be provided for the realization of the planned design.

239514.explanation: This is a complex issue. Good for your wallet, but municipal services may come under pressure. The municipality can also look at its own expenses. Some expenditures are unnecessary (expensive).

239515.explanation: Tourist tax as a revenue model? The Executive Board found that the costs of monitoring visitor numbers did not outweigh the revenue. Starting point: honesty of B&B owners and 'others.' Complex. Look at the dog tax; 'abolished'; it has other social added value.

239516.explanation: The municipality should not get involved in this. Landsmeer is not very large and has no large industrial areas. For work, it is a commuter village. Just peace, living, and recreation. And no traffic-attracting companies or a regional function without accompanying infrastructure.

239517.explanation: Landsmeer has no space for that. Priority lies in housing and livability.

239518.explanation: Historically, farming was a priority. However, times have changed. From intensive livestock farming, export, and economic interests, priorities shift toward livability and healthy living for humans and animals. Any sensible person can understand that in the general interest.

239519.explanation: Farming mainly takes place in the rural area and could also innovate. Positief Landsmeer finds the word "always" in the statement too absolute. No rights should be derived from it.

239522.explanation: Although there are sometimes heartbreaking cases, waiting times for home seekers are very long. There was even mention of 22 years for a social rental home. So simply get at the back of the queue. In emergencies, other solutions are always possible.

239523.explanation: In Landsmeer, this is partly a task of the national government and otherwise depends on cooperation from many volunteers from Landsmeer. The municipality can facilitate, for example, by offering spaces for conversations.

239524.explanation: Nothing lasts forever. Poor combustion causes odor nuisance. The coziness and warmth for one cannot lead to smoke nuisance for another. That completely depends on the situation. In rural areas, there will be less smoke nuisance than in densely built environments.

239525.explanation: Maintenance costs money. That saves the municipality money. Additionally, the advantage is that the neighborhood can decorate their own surroundings (if they are able) themselves with flowers and such. Then you can get a beautiful and friendly municipality. Good agreements must be made.

239526.explanation: Landsmeer is not very large. There isn’t much space, except perhaps in the office sector and at home. But that should not come at the expense of quality of life and must not attract additional traffic.

239527.explanation: That does not have to be just decorative fireworks. A lot can be done with laser shows and music (drums). A traditional end-of-year celebration experienced together as a community seems good. Immediately beautiful wishes for the new year.

239528.explanation: This is an unmotivated statement. It depends on your perspective. It depends on the location. If the solar panels are on tall poles (with enough space between them), people can park their cars underneath and thus hide them from view. You can also allow natural tree growth.

239529.explanation: It is a bit more nuanced. No large traffic-attracting festivals, at least not within the municipality of Landsmeer.

239531.explanation: Yes, in this watery area learning to swim is essential. However, school swimming should always be organized in consultation with primary schools and the possibilities of swimming pools.

239533.explanation: But then a different system. On-call when needed and not based on hours. Being available 24 hours a day is too much and too expensive. Currently, the enforcement officers have 20 hours per week and that is not enough. Social control during evening hours 7/24 is sometimes necessary.

239534.explanation: Unfortunately, it is necessary at this time. For places where nuisance arises from gatherings and excessive noise. Then the enforcement officer can monitor multiple locations as well. Killing several birds with one stone.

239535.explanation: Yes, in Landsmeer there are 75% owner-occupied homes and therefore 25% rental homes. That should be balanced a bit more.

239536.explanation: This partly depends on the environment. The village character is low-rise buildings. The character and living environment are decisive. So not close to Dorpsstraat and Ilperveld as currently planned. Possibly terraced along the ring road as a noise buffer; with the elevated A10 ring road, it will not be a disturbing block.

239537.explanation: Yes, given the housing shortage, that is necessary. And also to prevent undesirable effects.

239539.explanation: Not with subsidy from the municipality. Commercial buildings can be converted, if the situation allows, but at the cost and risk of the project developer. This to alleviate a small part of the housing shortage.

239540.explanation: There are not many building opportunities. We have a lot of natural areas which must be preserved. Only between the southern buildings of Landsmeer and the A10 ring road are there possibilities on both sides of the Zuideinde.

239541.explanation: To relieve the housing shortage, a lot can be gained by a combination of using flexible housing (temporary accommodation) and utilizing existing space through housing conversion (splitting/sharing).

239542.explanation: The municipality can set rules by ordinance. The municipality may not pursue income policy. If necessary, the municipality must assist individual cases. That cannot be expressed with a general statement.

239543.explanation: Receiving social assistance is not without reason. If people are unable due to physical disability, the word "must" is unjustifiably stigmatizing. Besides, people cannot always stay idle. So volunteer work is recommended.

239545.explanation: Parking takes up a lot of space. From the question you can infer there are too few parking spaces. The current blue zone does prevent long-term parking. We must not create a ‘waterbed effect’. If the shopping center aims for a regional function, that's another story.

239547.explanation: Parking is simply a problem in busy neighborhoods with dense buildings and around shopping centers. We see the danger of a 'waterbed effect': shifting the problems. Landsmeer is not very large. This requires an integrated approach, also in combination with bus stops and neighboring municipalities.

239549.explanation: Youth are the future. Additionally, the municipality, in collaboration with parents and youth work, should establish a youth center that is so attractive that it becomes a must for young people to spend their time there and enjoy themselves with peers.

239550.explanation: A city card lowers the threshold for many more contacts. Its possibilities are underestimated. It more easily opens doors to cultural experiences, music, and museums. You can share it with friends and it provides an opportunity to do things together; reducing loneliness!

239556.explanation: But more for a community garden with flowers to sit in during nice weather, as an extension of your garden. That can improve livability and strengthen neighborhood feeling. A subsidy as an encouragement stimulus. Adoption and maintenance/care then make more sense.

239557.explanation: Utilizing existing space through housing conversion (splitting/sharing) is an option. Municipalities play a central role by issuing urgency statements, combating vacancy, and easing rules for splitting homes to quickly create more living space.

239558.explanation: Society is so complicated that a helpdesk in the form of a social counselor may be desirable. This person can also refer further. Quickly solving problems prevents worsening. It should be easily accessible.

239559.explanation: But then by good combining and developing. Positief Landsmeer has been advocating for years the idea of a soundproof, terraced building along the west side of the A10. So far, no political majority has been found for this. Incomprehensible. We could reduce housing shortage and counter noise.

239560.explanation: Reducing air traffic will not succeed. That is national policy. Then the Netherlands would have to build an airport at sea. Sometimes, during busy times and depending on wind direction, planes take routes over Landsmeer when taking off and landing. Pushing with good arguments can help.

239561.explanation: Personal responsibility for your own health? How big is the group pressure and temptation? Education is better than banning. Who makes the moral choice? A snack sells well at the club. Income versus sensitive issues such as obesity, smoking, and (excessive) alcohol use.

239564.explanation: How the extra investments lead to faster handling of reports is the question. Often parts have to be ordered for replacement. For nuisance, enforcement officers should get more time in evening hours and/or the police should be able/willing to prioritize. It is not a quick report.

239565.explanation: The infrastructure is currently inadequate. In emergencies everything is jammed. Also with extra housing construction, proper access is absolutely necessary. Because there are major housing plans east and west of the Zuideinde, this is unavoidable. Also, another entrance/access must be created.

239511.explanation: A referendum is not the right instrument for expressing an opinion, because then nothing can be adjusted anymore. Politics must ensure that the opinion of residents is known through public consultation (e.g., surveys, participation, etc.) and develop their plans accordingly.

239512.explanation: Fine. There are already good experiences with it and it ensures an involved municipality and connection between residents.

239513.explanation: Of course, this depends on the plans and costs, but as a starting point it is good that residents have involvement in their neighborhood and where possible can work themselves on its improvement.

239514.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is one of the few taxes that the municipality can directly influence. Therefore, it should be used sparingly. The property tax contributes to a healthy Landsmeer.

239515.explanation: Due to national tax increases, entrepreneurs in this sector are already greatly disadvantaged. More taxes are not a good development. However, the municipality should focus on better collection of the tourist tax, because that is still insufficient.

239516.explanation: Landsmeer is already very attractive, without a campaign from the municipality. Money can be better spent in another way.

239517.explanation: There is more demand for housing than for business parks. Better use of the currently available business parks is more obvious.

239518.explanation: Farmers who want to should be able to continue farming their land. But if farmers want to stop, the land can be given another destination.

239519.explanation: Under conditions, this should be possible, but not always. The CDA believes it should not apply to every farmer, because in some places the municipality, for example, does not want a campsite or shop.

239522.explanation: The CDA believes that municipalities must retain the freedom to prioritize vulnerable groups at their discretion. With the dispersion law, municipalities are asked to take responsibility by contributing to asylum seeker housing in the region.

239523.explanation: The faster newcomers integrate, the faster they can contribute to Dutch society. Investment in integration is therefore an investment in a better future.

239524.explanation: This must take into account the environment and weather conditions. The municipality could issue guidelines to inform residents about this.

239525.explanation: Of course, this depends on the plans and costs, but as a starting point it is good that residents have involvement in their neighborhood and where possible can work themselves on its improvement.

239526.explanation: The municipality must ensure a good business climate and clear establishment requirements. However, there must be equality between companies already established in Landsmeer and new businesses. It cannot be that there is no regulatory equality. However, companies can be helped to become more sustainable.

239527.explanation: Great plan. Turn it into a nice celebration at a well-visible location. Additionally, enforce laws properly regarding the illegal use of fireworks.

239528.explanation: In consultation with the municipality, places should be found where this can be done and is allowed.

239529.explanation: Definitely. Preferably no festivals at all, since there are already so many other great locations for that. The Twiske is a beautiful piece of nature that should be cherished in its current form.

239531.explanation: Organizationally and financially, this has proven very difficult. However, we do support stimulating swimming for those with limited financial means, for example by contributing to costs.

239533.explanation: Spending community funds must be sensible, economical, and careful. Focus should be on better enforcement, but we do not think 24-hour deployment solves the underlying problem. More education, prevention, and neighborhood apps can also make a substantial contribution.

239534.explanation: We don't want 'big brother,' but we do want supervision in places where it adds value. For example, in terms of traffic safety or safety on the streets near bars and shops.

239535.explanation: This is financially unfeasible. 30% seems more realistic. However, more housing turnover and a start on the housing market for young people must be ensured. But that does not involve 40% social rental housing.

239536.explanation: Building must fit the environment, but we must make optimal use of space. So if four stories are possible, we should do that.

239537.explanation: The housing market is already too distorted by investors. Furthermore, national regulations have already been made for this and are increasingly implemented at the municipal level.

239539.explanation: Commercial buildings are owned by the owner. They must bear the costs.

239540.explanation: The housing shortage is so high that, within predetermined frameworks, housing construction outside the built-up area must be possible.

239541.explanation: Certainly, this can make an important contribution to housing market turnover and give starters/students a chance.

239542.explanation: Poverty in a country like the Netherlands is actually unnecessary, provided we distribute the money properly. More help for those in need is then a good thing. Caring for others. Additionally, the municipality must help people become self-sufficient so they can escape poverty.

239543.explanation: Not everyone on welfare can do something about it independently. Compulsory counter-performance is good in itself, but the person must be able to do it. So encouraging someone to reach that point is better than forcing them.

239545.explanation: Parking spaces have recently been added. Good enforcement of parking times will have more effect than adding even more parking spaces. This land is needed for other purposes.

239547.explanation: This does not solve the underlying problem. The parking issue is made even more difficult by the spillover effect of the recently introduced parking permit policy in Amsterdam-North. There is no easy solution for this.

239549.explanation: This age group especially benefits from attention and help in development and self-realization. This may also help prevent nuisance.

239556.explanation: Not everything can be shifted onto the municipality. Ultimately, we must share the burdens.

239557.explanation: This can be a solution for the housing shortage.

239558.explanation: If there is more demand in this area, it must be facilitated.

239559.explanation: Yes, because that is still an obligation resulting from the construction of Luijendijk-Zuid.

239560.explanation: Actions are already being taken especially regarding night flights. Further prevention of nuisance caused by air traffic is encouraged.

239561.explanation: A healthy mind in a healthy body. Advertisements are proven to be tempting, and 'what does not go on, does not have to come off.'

239564.explanation: Currently, the Fixi app already works well and reports are being addressed more quickly. If investing in more staff, careful consideration must be given to how that will be funded. In short, what will we do 'less' so that we can pay for more staff?

239565.explanation: Eventually, a second access road will be needed for Landsmeer, also to be used by other road users. Green space will have to be sacrificed for this.

239511.explanation: We consider the involvement of our residents important. They must be heard on important decisions.

239512.explanation: Basic maintenance of public spaces remains a municipal task.

239513.explanation: Involvement of our residents is important.

239514.explanation: It must be examined whether a reduction of burdens for our residents is possible.

239515.explanation: In case of budget deficits, raising the tourist tax can be a good alternative.

239516.explanation: The scarce resources can be better spent than for an effect that is not proven.

239517.explanation: By new construction, we mean the building of homes.

239518.explanation: Compulsory is not good, but where there is a desire, it must be possible.

239519.explanation: It can be a good alternative for some, but it is not possible everywhere.

239522.explanation: If we truly focus on public housing, prioritization among home seekers is unnecessary. Current policy builds too many expensive owner-occupied homes and pits people against each other for scarce social rentals. We choose more social rental homes and tackling waiting times.

239523.explanation: It is about treating these people decently and encouraging them to participate in our community.

239524.explanation: Although it is a nice atmospheric moment, it is a health risk for many people.

239525.explanation: Neighborhood participation must be encouraged, but green maintenance is an important municipal task.

239526.explanation: Sustainability must be a top priority, but it can be an impossible task for small businesses.

239527.explanation: When society calls for it, consideration can be given to when this is financially feasible.

239528.explanation: Although the green landscape must be preserved, initiatives may arise where it should be possible.

239529.explanation: It is an important nature reserve that must be protected and of which, unfortunately, we have very few left.

239531.explanation: We live in a watery environment where being able to swim is vital.

239533.explanation: Enforcement of our public spaces is important. Community enforcement officers don't need to be present continuously, but they should be available occasionally in the evenings and at night.

239534.explanation: Camera surveillance creates a false sense of security. Safety is achieved through police visibility, enforcement, and social control.

239535.explanation: People reliant on social rental housing face the longest waiting times. For those with deep pockets, there is no housing crisis.

239536.explanation: If we deviate from this, it must be suitable for the location and widely supported by local residents.

239537.explanation: A residency obligation is a tool to combat weekend tourists and absentee landlords exploiting the housing market.

239539.explanation: Municipal subsidies for private developers are unnecessary. Construction projects can financially support themselves well.

239540.explanation: The municipality must commit to feasible locations.

239541.explanation: In general, the municipality must ensure that single-family homes remain available for families.

239542.explanation: Many people fall through the cracks.

239543.explanation: Everyone is expected to contribute to society according to their ability. Not everyone manages this, for example, a single mother on welfare who has a busy life caring well for her children.

239545.explanation: There are enough parking spaces around the shopping center.

239547.explanation: There are already enough alternatives like blue zones. Introducing parking permits will impose extra costs on people.

239549.explanation: Good contact with young people is essential for the future.

239550.explanation: Through the community center, seniors can already participate in enjoyable activities. This is good to prevent loneliness.

239556.explanation: The opportunity must be offered, but the costs are the responsibility of the initiator.

239557.explanation: Single-family homes must remain available for families.

239558.explanation: People should be able to easily approach the social counselor with all kinds of questions.

239559.explanation: The municipality must advocate for this, but the responsibility lies with Rijkswaterstaat (the national water authority).

239560.explanation: Unrestricted growth of Schiphol Airport is not possible.

239561.explanation: A healthy society begins with protecting our youth from undue influence.

239564.explanation: Good communication with the municipality is important for the quality of our public spaces.

239565.explanation: Legally, this is not allowed in nature reserves and it involves high costs. Let's focus the discussion on the important matters.

239511.explanation: DorpsBelang explicitly supports referenda for important decisions, such as administrative mergers and other major issues.

239512.explanation: Initiatives from residents that contribute to social cohesion, livability, and involvement fit the village concept, but taking over municipal tasks must never be done for budget cuts or at the expense of employment.

239513.explanation: DorpsBelang believes resident initiatives strengthen social cohesion and align with the principle: by, for, through, and together with the residents. Initiatives are reviewed and assessed on a project basis.

239514.explanation: DorpsBelang strives for the lowest possible housing costs.

239515.explanation: DorpsBelang finds it important that the municipality actually collects the tourist tax and implements more controls. Tourism must not come at the expense of livability. Therefore, DorpsBelang only supports small-scale tourism. Dutch residents are not tourists to us, but they are good for the economy.

239516.explanation: DorpsBelang focuses on local entrepreneurs, not on active acquisition or business growth. Landsmeer has a serious shortage of affordable housing. DorpsBelang considers housing construction its top priority.

239517.explanation: Additional business parks do not fit the village character and the currently limited infrastructure. We want to prioritize housing construction.

239518.explanation: Farmers and horticulturists are very important and part of our cultural heritage. DorpsBelang is against any form of coercion or loss of control for landowners. Where there is no succession, it will naturally cease and the land could potentially be converted to nature reserves or housing.

239519.explanation: DorpsBelang wants tailored solutions and clear rules so equal treatment is possible; therefore, no automatic permission for farmers to open a shop or campsite at their business. Unfair competition can be a consideration.

239522.explanation: DorpsBelang wants maximum priority for local residents within legal frameworks. The introduction of the points system gives housing seekers more equal opportunities. Villagers wishing to continue living in their village have fewer chances because there are few homes to allocate.

239523.explanation: DorpsBelang believes good integration contributes to social cohesion, promotes equal opportunities, and a safe inclusive community. However, DorpsBelang wants to prioritize its own residents in the coming period due to, among other things, a large shortage of housing and financial resources.

239524.explanation: Health and sustainability are important, but DorpsBelang chooses reasonableness and tailored approaches without excessive paternalism.

239525.explanation: DorpsBelang believes public green spaces should be maintained by the municipality itself. Residents pay for this. If neighborhood residents want to contribute themselves, that is possible.

239526.explanation: Sustainability is important, but DorpsBelang opts for realism and tailored approaches. Businesses should be encouraged to become more sustainable without rigid admission criteria that hinder local business activity.

239527.explanation: DorpsBelang prefers to focus on safety, peace, and local alternatives rather than large-scale fireworks. Amsterdam (the IJ) is nearby and an excellent place to watch big fireworks displays.

239528.explanation: Meadows are part of the landscape. DorpsBelang does not want solar panels in meadows, but on roofs.

239529.explanation: DorpsBelang wants fewer commercial and large-scale festivals in Het Twiske to protect nature and tranquility. Het Twiske is a Natura 2000 area and not a festival site. The infrastructure and logistics make Het Twiske unsuitable. Parts of Het Twiske will be closed for extended periods.

239531.explanation: DorpsBelang finds it important that children have equal opportunities and that swimming safety aligns with youth care, especially in a water-rich area like Landsmeer.

239533.explanation: DorpsBelang advocates for more enforcement and visibility, also outside office hours. More safety and fewer burglaries.

239534.explanation: DorpsBelang supports camera surveillance on access roads, but also at Purmerlanderrijweg, Kanaaldijk, Polderweg, and in the sports park. Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and prevention.

239535.explanation: DorpsBelang explicitly aims to realize at least 40% social rental housing on municipal land, but prefers more than 40% so that young people can save to buy afterwards. This is good for housing flow.

239536.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit the village feeling and harm the village and rural character. In Landsmeer, there was already opposition to high-rise during the environmental vision participation. That refers to three floors. So more than four stories is, of course, very undesirable!

239537.explanation: DorpsBelang supports an owner-occupancy obligation for new construction on municipal land but does not want a general obligation for all owner-occupied homes in the municipality. It can also be a home for parents or children, then it may be different. We oppose housing withdrawal for holiday homes or Airbnb.

239539.explanation: DorpsBelang favors reuse of existing buildings. This fits with smart and sustainable use of space, but DorpsBelang believes the developer should arrange financing themselves.

239540.explanation: The infrastructure in Landsmeer hardly allows for infill development. DorpsBelang wants to build outside the existing built-up area, especially on municipal land in the west and east sides of Het Lint. This way, we want to spare the village centers and the historic Lint and protect the village character.

239541.explanation: DorpsBelang wants an expansion of house sharing and hospitaverhuur under clear conditions. It must remain limited and not lead to overcrowding. Setting rules is necessary to prevent nuisance (housing permit, fire safety, etc.).

239542.explanation: DorpsBelang wants to tackle poverty and silent poverty but opts for tailored solutions and good signaling instead of a generic widening of income limits. Special circumstances, such as illness, may require additional financial support.

239543.explanation: We do encourage participation. Being involved is important, but DorpsBelang opposes coercion without customization. Illness may prevent someone from participating.

239545.explanation: DorpsBelang is in favor of local purchasing and finds it important that the shopping center is easily accessible. DorpsBelang wants to maintain the blue zone and permit holders. Additionally, DorpsBelang supports more and better bicycle parking facilities so residents can do their daily shopping by bike.

239547.explanation: Permits can limit parking pressure in residential neighborhoods and increase livability in the immediate surroundings.

239549.explanation: More youth work contributes to prevention, safety, and development, but the number of hours is tailored and depends on the goal.

239550.explanation: Older people should be able to continue participating in culture and society. We would also like to see a city pass for residents with a minimum income.

239556.explanation: DorpsBelang believes that resident initiatives strengthen social cohesion and align with the principle: from, for, by, and together with residents. Initiatives are reviewed on a project basis.

239557.explanation: Dividing and sharing can alleviate housing shortages, provided it concerns local residents and, for example, students, not-for example-migrant workers.

239558.explanation: Accessible help prevents problems and aligns with good service.

239559.explanation: DorpsBelang finds it incredible that the money, which the municipality should have reserved from the proceeds of building Luijendijk-Zuid for the sound barriers, has disappeared. DorpsBelang wants homes on the municipal land in Entrée West. The proceeds from that can close the gap.

239560.explanation: Less air traffic improves residents' quality of life and health, but residents recently indicated on Local Broadcast Landsmeer that they are not bothered by air traffic. It's best to distribute noise and annoyance over as many flight routes as possible.

239561.explanation: DorpsBelang considers health important but is cautious about general prohibitions. Awareness and education are preferred over extensive regulation.

239564.explanation: Rapid follow-up of reports increases trust and livability. So far adequate; otherwise, extra compliance checks are needed.

239565.explanation: A second access road is essential to relieve the Lint. However, the location of the road is crucial and should not end at Zuideinde but directly connect to the A10 exit. DorpsBelang wants to know the road's location first before indicating whether they are for or against.

statements.title: The municipality of Landsmeer should appeal to the national government and Schiphol for reduced air traffic.
The residents of Landsmeer should continue to be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Landsmeer moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen blijven geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.theme: Parking spaces at the shopping center
statements.title: There should be more parking spaces at the shopping center in Landsmeer.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents should be able to continue taking over the management of a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign marketing Landsmeer as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.
The location where a company establishes itself.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can attract companies by showing what they have to offer. This can be done, for example, with a promotional campaign. The goal is to convince companies to settle in the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should work towards creating a road for cars through the Landsmeerderveld nature area.
At least 40% of newly built homes in the municipality of Landsmeer should be social rental housing. Currently, 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente Landsmeer moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
statements.moreInfo: Farmers need a lot of land for their businesses. That land is usually their own property, but they cannot do whatever they want with it. The municipality determines the function of land in a zoning plan. The purpose of a piece of land can be, for example, housing, shops or agriculture. If a farmer wants to do something else on land zoned for agriculture, the municipality must change the zoning plan. This applies to all activities that are not agriculture, such as building houses, running a farm shop, operating a campsite or a daycare.

statements.title: Old commercial buildings should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow fewer festivals in Het Twiske.
Landsmeer should have municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who can be deployed 24 hours a day.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die 24 uur per dag inzetbaar zijn.

statements.title: There should be more youth work hours available in the municipality; currently that is 20 hours per week.

titleParts.glossary: People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

titleParts.text: beschikbaar zijn in de gemeente. Nu is dat 20 uur per week.

statements.title: The municipality should spend money on better noise barriers along the A10 motorway.
The municipality should ban solar panels on pastures/fields.
The municipality should reintroduce a city pass for people aged 67 and over that provides discounts on cultural activities.

With the Fixi app residents can report issues to the municipality, for example about litter, nuisance and vandalism. The municipality should invest in additional staff so reports are handled more quickly.

titleParts.glossary: Abbreviation for application, usually referring to a program on a smartphone.

titleParts.text: kunnen inwoners meldingen doorgeven aan de gemeente, bijvoorbeeld over zwerfvuil, overlast en vernielingen. De gemeente moet geld steken in extra medewerkers zodat meldingen sneller worden opgepakt.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Landsmeer.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Residents who want to create neighborhood or front-yard gardens should receive funding from the municipality.
The municipality should oppose new development outside the built-up area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet nieuwbouw buiten de

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The social welfare counselor helps residents with questions about, for example, benefits or allowances. There should be more walk-in consultation hours with the social counselor.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should ban fast food advertising in bus shelters, swimming pools and sports halls.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Landsmeer should introduce parking permits in the residential neighborhoods around the center.

titleParts.text: Landsmeer moet in de woonwijken rondom het centrum

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should not allow agricultural land in Landsmeer to be converted into nature areas.
statements.moreInfo: Farmers can receive money from the national government and the province to convert agricultural land into nature. This mainly applies to farmland that borders nature reserves. The municipality decides together with the province where new nature is desired. For this the municipality must amend the Environmental Plan (Omgevingsplan).

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.

shootoutStatements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.
Residents who have a plan to improve their neighborhood should continue to receive funding from the municipality.
More business parks should be developed in Landsmeer.
The municipality should make funds available to citizens who want to maintain the greenery in their neighborhood themselves.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
Apartment buildings of more than four storeys should be allowed in Landsmeer.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Landsmeer.
Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
People receiving social assistance should do socially useful work in return, for example at a community center or a sports club.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance. More people would then receive help.
The municipality should allow dividing or splitting single-family homes.
